title: operculina turpethum
intro: Operculina turpethum, also known as Amukkara or Turpeth, is a plant native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia, classified as a type of vining climber in the Convolvulaceae family. It has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Operculina turpethum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve symptoms of nausea and vomiting, and exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Operculina turpethum is often used in the form of decoctions, powder, or oil, and can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
